study_title: The genetic underpinnings of Varroa mite and disease resistance in honey bees
design: Honey bees were collected in two batches from the brood box of standard Langstroth hives in wooden cages, where each cage consisted of 150300 bees, and transported to the laboratory. The boxes flash-frozen in liquid nitrogen and then stored at 80 C upon arrival. Whole-bee homogenates were made by macerating 100150 frozen honey bees per sampled colony in 15 mL of DEPC-treated water. RNA extraction was performed using the EcoPURE Total RNA Kit (ECOTECH Biotechnology, Erzurum, Turkey) according to the manufacturers protocol on 150 L of RNA per sample. The lysis buffer from the kit was mixed with -mercaptoethanol in a volume ratio of 100:1. An on-column DNase I treatment (EURx, Gdask, Poland) step was applied during RNA extraction according to the manufacturers protocol. Preliminary assessment of total RNA yield and purity, and integrity was done using NanoDrop 1000c spectrophotometer (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Waltham, MA, USA). RNA integrity was assessed by separation via electrophoresis for 35 min on 1% agarose gels mixed with 0.5% household bleach (6% sodium hypochlorite) prior to melting, and the Agilent 2100 Bioanalyzer with the RNA 6000 Nano Kit (Agilent Technologies, Santa Clara, CA, USA) according to the manufacturers protocol without performing heat denaturation on RNA samples. First-strand cDNA libraries from captured mRNA were constructed and sequenced on Illumina NovaSeq 6000 (paired-end, 150 bp).
